name: PM-KISAN (Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi)
eligibility: Small and marginal landholding farmer families. Excludes income-tax payees, institutional landholders, and constitutional post holders.
benefits: Rs. 6,000 per year, paid in 3 installments of Rs. 2,000 directly to the farmer's bank account.
how_to_apply: Register at pmkisan.gov.in, via a Common Service Centre (CSC), or the local agriculture/revenue officer with land records and Aadhaar.

name: PMFBY (Pradhan Mantri Fasal Bima Yojana)
eligibility: All farmers (loanee and non-loanee) growing notified crops in notified areas.
benefits: Crop insurance against yield loss from natural calamities, pests, or disease, at low premium (about 2% kharif, 1.5% rabi, 5% commercial/horticulture crops).
how_to_apply: Loanee farmers are auto-enrolled by their bank unless they opt out. Others can apply via a bank, CSC, or the national crop insurance portal before the season's cutoff date.

name: Soil Health Card Scheme
benefits: Free soil testing and a Soil Health Card every 2 years with nutrient status and fertilizer/crop recommendations.
how_to_apply: Contact the local agriculture department office or nearest Krishi Vigyan Kendra to get soil samples tested and a card issued.

name: Kisan Credit Card (KCC)
eligibility: Farmers, tenant farmers, sharecroppers, and SHGs/JLGs of farmers, including those in animal husbandry and fisheries.
benefits: Short-term credit for crop and post-harvest expenses at subsidized interest (effective around 4% with timely repayment).
how_to_apply: Apply at any nearby bank branch (public, private, cooperative, or regional rural bank) with land and identity documents, or via the bank's online portal.

name: MGNREGA (Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act)
eligibility: Adult members of rural households willing to do unskilled manual work.
benefits: Legal guarantee of 100 days of wage employment per financial year per household at the notified minimum wage.
how_to_apply: Apply for a Job Card at the Gram Panchayat office with address proof and photos, then submit a written work request.

name: PMKVY (Pradhan Mantri Kaushal Vikas Yojana)
eligibility: Indian youth, especially school/college dropouts or unemployed persons seeking industry-relevant skills.
benefits: Free short-term skill training with certification, plus a monetary reward on successful assessment.
how_to_apply: Enroll at a nearby PMKVY-affiliated training centre or through the Skill India portal (pmkvyofficial.org).

name: Ayushman Bharat / PM-JAY
eligibility: Poor and vulnerable families identified via SECC 2011 deprivation/occupation criteria; no cap on family size or age.
benefits: Health cover of Rs. 5 lakh per family per year for secondary and tertiary hospitalization at empanelled hospitals, cashless and paperless.
how_to_apply: Check eligibility at pmjay.gov.in or with an Ayushman Mitra at an empanelled hospital/CSC; eligible families receive an Ayushman Card.

name: PMAY-Urban (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Urban)
eligibility: Urban poor households (EWS/LIG/MIG categories) without a pucca house anywhere in India.
benefits: Interest subsidy on home loans for construction, purchase, or enhancement of a house (up to about Rs. 2.67 lakh depending on income category).
how_to_apply: Apply online at pmaymis.gov.in, or through a Common Service Centre or the local Urban Local Body office with Aadhaar and income proof.

name: PMAY-Gramin (Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana - Gramin)
eligibility: Rural houseless families and those living in kutcha/dilapidated houses, identified via the SECC 2011/Awaas+ survey.
benefits: Rs. 1.20 lakh (plain areas) or Rs. 1.30 lakh (hilly/difficult areas) to build a pucca house, often combined with MGNREGA labour support and toilet scheme convergence.
how_to_apply: Check the PMAY-G beneficiary list, or register via the Awaas+ survey at the Gram Panchayat/Block office.

name: NSAP Old Age Pension (Indira Gandhi National Old Age Pension Scheme)
eligibility: BPL individuals aged 60 years and above.
benefits: Monthly pension: Rs. 200-300 (age 60-79) or Rs. 500 (80+) from the centre; many states add a top-up.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Gram Panchayat/Urban Local Body or district Social Welfare Office with age and BPL proof.

name: NSAP Widow Pension (Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme)
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs. 300 from the centre; many states add a top-up.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Gram Panchayat/Urban Local Body or district Social Welfare Office with proof of widowhood and BPL status.

name: NSAP Disability Pension (Indira Gandhi National Disability Pension Scheme)
eligibility: BPL persons aged 18-79 with 80%+ of a single disability or multiple disabilities.
benefits: Monthly pension of Rs. 300 (or Rs. 500 for age 80+) from the centre; many states add a top-up.
how_to_apply: Apply at the Gram Panchayat/Urban Local Body or district Social Welfare Office with a disability certificate and BPL proof.

name: Beti Bachao Beti Padhao
eligibility: Nationwide awareness and welfare scheme for the girl child, with focus on gender-critical districts.
benefits: Awareness campaigns and incentives to improve child sex ratio and girls' education, often linked with the Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana savings scheme.
how_to_apply: Implemented via the district Women & Child Development office; for the linked savings account, open a Sukanya Samriddhi account at a bank or post office for a girl child under age 10.

name: ICDS / Anganwadi Services (Integrated Child Development Services)
eligibility: Children under 6 years, pregnant and lactating mothers, and adolescent girls.
benefits: Supplementary nutrition, immunization referrals, health check-ups, and pre-school non-formal education.
how_to_apply: Register at the nearest Anganwadi Centre in your locality.

name: DAY-NRLM / Self-Help Groups (National Rural Livelihood Mission)
eligibility: Rural poor women willing to organize into Self-Help Groups (SHGs).
benefits: Access to bank credit at subsidized interest, revolving fund support, and skill/livelihood training for SHG members.
how_to_apply: Join or form an SHG through the local NRLM/DAY-NRLM block office or an existing SHG federation.

name: Post-Matric Scholarship (SC/ST/OBC/Minority students)
eligibility: SC/ST/OBC/Minority community students studying post-matriculation (class 11 onwards) with family income below the prescribed limit (varies by category/state).
benefits: Reimbursement of tuition fees and a maintenance allowance for the duration of the course.
how_to_apply: Apply through the National Scholarship Portal (scholarships.gov.in) with caste certificate, income certificate, and institution verification.
